item.title: FSIS Issues Public Health Alert for Ready-To-Eat Beef Jerky Products Due to Misbranding and Undeclared Allergen
item.product: FSIS Issues Public Health Alert for Ready-To-Eat Beef Jerky Products Due to Misbranding and Undeclared Allergen
item.summary: Punahele Jerky Company, Inc. Active Sun, 03/01/2026 - Current Nationwide WASHINGTON, March 1, 2026 – The U.S. Department of Agriculture's Food Safety and Inspection Service (FSIS) is issuing a public health alert for beef jerky products due to misbranding and undeclared allergens. The products may contain soy lecithin, a known allergen, which is not declared... Impacted Products 6-oz. plastic packages containing "DRIED HAWAIIAN STYLE BEEF CRISPS, ORIGINAL SALT &amp; PEPPER FLAVOR", and bearing establishment number "EST. 2625" inside the USDA mark of inspection. 4-oz. plastic packages containing "PUNAHELE JERKY COMPANY INC UNCLE K'S BEEF CRISPS". 4-oz. p
item.affectedPeople: Consumers who purchased the affected meat, poultry, or egg product.
item.remedy: Do not consume affected products; follow USDA FSIS recall or public health alert instructions.
item.payoutAmount: Refund, disposal, or return instructions if stated by the official notice
